Sanraku Japanese Cuisine

Sanraku Japanese Cuisine (Oakridge)

← Back to all restaurants
★★★★★ 4.5
📍 San Jose, California📅 2026
Japanese Sushi Raw Tempura Izakaya
Sanraku is a legendary name in the San Francisco Bay Area’s Japanese food scene, and their San Jose branch tucked inside Westfield Oakridge is a total gem. The whole experience centers around an open sushi bar where you can watch the chefs expertly hand-press nigiri and craft Edomae-style dishes (a traditional Tokyo-style preparation) right before your eyes. If you’re looking for a legit spot for fresh sashimi and authentic Japanese flavors while out shopping, this is your place. The nigiri lineup is honestly stunning: the Otoro (fatty tuna) has that perfect, delicate marbling, the seared salmon belly literally melts in your mouth, and the amberjack and white tuna are incredibly clean and fresh. They even serve the sweet shrimp with crispy fried shrimp heads so you get two textures in one go, plus some lovely sweet-and-savory Inari (sushi rice tucked into fried tofu skins). For small plates, the charcoal-grilled whole squid is packed with savory sauce, and the crispy calamari legs karaage (Japanese-style deep fry) paired with mayo is a crowd-pleaser. You can also grab a refreshing sashimi salad topped with roe and shredded daikon. If you want something heartier, they’ve got Donburi (rice bowls) like Salmon & Ikura or Eel Tempura, and the Tempura Udon is served with a basket of crispy shrimp and veggies perched right over the steaming noodles. Perfect for a mid-shopping break, casual catch-ups, and anyone craving fresh sushi or authentic Japanese cuisine for lunch or dinner.
Good for A mid-shopping break Casual catch-ups

What I ordered

Fatty Tuna Nigiri (Otoro) Seared Salmon Belly Nigiri (Aburi Salmon Belly) Salmon Nigiri (Sake) Amberjack Nigiri (Kanpachi) White Tuna Nigiri (Shiro Maguro) Sweet Shrimp Nigiri (with Fried Shrimp Head; Amaebi) Inari Sushi (Fried Tofu Pouch) Charcoal-Grilled Whole Squid Crispy Fried Squid Tentacles (Karaage) Sashimi Salad (Sashimi Sarada) Tempura Udon Shrimp Tempura Roll (Ebi Tempura Roll) Salmon and Ikura Don (Salmon and Roe Bowl; Sake Oyako Don) Eel Tempura Don (Unagi Tempura Bowl)

Dishes
